# Installer (delivery machinery)

Covers the two installation paths that put the skill and/or slash commands into a host agent: the zero-dep Node CLI (`bin/create-loop.js`, Path A) and the shell copier (`install-commands.sh`, Path B), plus the regression suite that guards them.

## Core Invariants

- The installer is zero-runtime-dependency (only Node stdlib `fs`/`path`/`os`/`crypto`) and requires Node `>=18` [source: bin/create-loop.js:43-46, package.json:29-31]. Never add an npm dependency to `bin/`.
- `writeManaged` is the single reconciliation path for every file the installer writes; it must return exactly one of `created`/`updated`/`unchanged`/`skipped-user`/`dry` [source: bin/create-loop.js:237-264].
- A file whose current on-disk hash differs from the hash the installer recorded when it last wrote it is treated as user-edited and preserved unless `--force` is passed [source: bin/create-loop.js:247-254].
- Install state (per-file sha256) lives OUTSIDE any host skill/command dir: global `~/.config/create-loop/install-state.json`, project `<proj>/.create-loop/install-state.json` [source: bin/create-loop.js:198-205].
- `install-commands.sh` (Path B) copies the ALREADY-RENDERED committed files from `.opencode/command/` and `.claude/commands/`; it does NOT render from `command/` [source: install-commands.sh:92-99, 114-124].

## Conventions

- Per-host behavior is table-driven: the `HOSTS` registry declares `skillDir`, `commandDir`, `detect`, and `renderCommand` for `opencode` and `claude` [source: bin/create-loop.js:73-121]. Add a host by adding a registry entry, not by branching logic elsewhere.
- Host auto-detection: a host is "detected" when its config dir already exists (`.opencode/` or `.claude/` for project scope) [source: bin/create-loop.js:88-91, 109-112]. `resolveHosts` honors explicit `--host`, else auto-detects, else falls back to all [source: bin/create-loop.js:284-291].
- CLI dispatch is a single `switch` in `main()` over install/uninstall/render/list/version/help, with aliases (`add`/`i`, `remove`/`rm`, `ls`) [source: bin/create-loop.js:543-554].
- Recursive skill copy excludes `.git`, `__pycache__`, `__pypackages__`, `node_modules` and the files `metadata.json`, `.DS_Store` [source: bin/create-loop.js:162-163].
- Skill files are copied verbatim; command files are rendered per host [source: bin/create-loop.js:314-336].

## Common Mistakes

- Editing rendered command files and expecting the installer to keep them — on the next non-`--force` run the installer will still overwrite an old-managed file, and a user edit is only preserved because its hash no longer matches the recorded one [source: bin/create-loop.js:241-254]. Edit `command/` and re-render instead (see [domains/commands.md](commands.md)).
- Assuming `install-commands.sh` renders — it only `cp`s pre-rendered files, so running it after editing `command/` without re-rendering ships stale commands [source: install-commands.sh:92-99].
- Passing both `--commands-only` and `--skill-only` — they are mutually exclusive and the parser fails fast [source: bin/create-loop.js:501].
- Expecting `render` to track state — `cmdRender` writes committed repo-root artifacts with NO install-state tracking, unlike `cmdInstall` [source: bin/create-loop.js:429-449 vs 293-364].

## Verified Facts

- `bin/create-loop.js` is 556 lines; key entry points: `cmdInstall` L293-364, `cmdUninstall` L366-407, `cmdRender` L429-449, `cmdList` L451-463, `parseArgs` L466-503 [source: bin/create-loop.js].
- The `MANIFEST_VERSION` for install-state is `1` [source: bin/create-loop.js:58].
- Uninstall removes only tracked files, preserves user-edited ones unless `--force`, and prunes emptied dirs upward toward the scope boundary [source: bin/create-loop.js:366-407, 420-426].
- The regression suite `test/installer.test.js` is 114 lines, 8 scenarios, 15 assertions, and passes 15/15 [source: test/installer.test.js:40-105; run 2026-07-03].
- Scenario 6 asserts `render` reproduces committed host command files byte-for-byte [source: test/installer.test.js:81-91].
- OpenCode skill target is `.agents/skills/create-loop` (project) / `~/.agents/skills/create-loop` (global), matching `npx skills add` so installs don't duplicate [source: bin/create-loop.js:79-82].

## Working Understanding

- The design deliberately mirrors the "standalone installer" pattern of BMAD-METHOD and spec-kit — a per-host adapter table + a bundled manifest as source of truth + idempotent re-run [source: bin/create-loop.js:10-14 comment]. Treat this as intentional prior art, not incidental.
- The `-y/--yes` flag is reserved/no-op because the installer is already non-interactive [source: bin/create-loop.js:33 comment].

## Open Questions

- Are hosts beyond `opencode` and `claude` planned? The registry is extensible but only two are wired [source: bin/create-loop.js:73-121]. [ASK USER]
- Does any consumer rely on the `--json` machine-readable output shape? Changing it could break automation [source: bin/create-loop.js:362]. [TODO] check for downstream usage.
